generations.generated_text:
Ellen marries Mr. O’Hara because she is heartbroken over the death of her cousin Philippe Robillard, who had been her love interest. She writes to him and expresses her desire to leave Savannah and marry Gerald O’Hara instead. The text states: "She sobbed till the dawn like a broken-hearted child... I will go away where I’ll never see them again... I will go away where I’ll never see them again, or this town, or anyone who reminds me of–of– him." The text also says: "It was the threat of the convent that finally won the assent of bewildered and heartstricken Pierre Robillard." This shows her decision is driven by grief and a desire to escape her past. 

The option that best reflects this is:

C. A miracle had happened.

This is because the text says: "Gerald himself never quite knew how it all came about. He only knew that a miracle had happened." This directly supports the answer.

C
